name: Unauthorized USB Device Detection
description: Detects unauthorized USB device connections on managed endpoints. When a USB insertion event is detected, the playbook checks the device against the approved device allowlist. Unauthorized devices trigger an immediate block action via the endpoint agent, create a policy violation ticket, and alert the SOC. Approved devices are logged and closed.
author: T1 Agentics

required_integrations.name: CrowdStrike Falcon
required_integrations.reason: Block unauthorized USB device and enforce device control policy

required_integrations.name: Jira
required_integrations.reason: Create policy violation ticket for unauthorized USB usage

config.description: Triggers when a USB device insertion event is detected on a managed endpoint

config.code:
device_id = alert.get('usb_device_id', '')
vendor_id = alert.get('usb_vendor_id', '')
approved = device_id in context.get('approved_usb_devices', []) or vendor_id in context.get('approved_usb_vendors', [])
result = {'approved': approved, 'device_id': device_id, 'vendor_id': vendor_id}
config.description: Checks the inserted USB device ID and vendor ID against the organization's approved device allowlist
Routes based on whether the USB device is on the approved allowlist

parameters.note: USB device {{code_1.device_id}} from vendor {{code_1.vendor_id}} is on the approved allowlist. No action required.

config.description: Log the approved device connection and close the alert
Block the unauthorized USB device via the endpoint agent's device control policy

parameters.title: Unauthorized USB Device — {{trigger_1.alert.hostname}}

config.description: Create a policy violation ticket in Jira for tracking and user follow-up
Notify the SOC team about the unauthorized USB device and actions taken
